skill.name: example-skill
skill.description: Template skill for defining automated Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows with placeholder capabilities, invocation rules, resources, and examples.
skill.summary: Template for building an automated AI workflow skill.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography detections at SKILL.md lines 3, 10, and 14-16 are false positives from generic template wording, not cryptographic code. Most backtick detections are Markdown inline code, but the skill allows Bash and instructs agents to run helper scripts that are not included for review, so publication should include a warning.

medium_findings.title: Unreviewable Helper Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash and instructs agents to execute helper scripts from a scripts directory, including validator.sh. The package only contains SKILL.md, so the referenced scripts cannot be reviewed for command injection, filesystem modification, or data exposure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and script-execution instructions are explicit. Confidence is below very high because no script body is present, so malicious behavior is not confirmed.

low_findings.title: Generic Placeholder Instructions
low_findings.description: The skill is an unfinished template with placeholder tasks, capabilities, and situations. This is not malicious, but it may cause broad or unexpected activation until the author replaces the placeholders.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The placeholder wording is directly visible across the skill file. It creates quality and activation concerns but does not show hostile intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The instructions tell agents to run helper scripts from the skill directory. External command execution should be limited to reviewed scripts with clear arguments and input validation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command-execution guidance is explicit and relevant to the declared Bash permission. The finding is a risk pattern rather than proof of malicious code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: Static detections at SKILL.md lines 30, 32, 33, 55, 58, 63, and 64 are Markdown inline code references, not Ruby shell backtick execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are clearly Markdown prose and resource references. No Ruby code or shell backtick execution syntax is present in context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: Static detections at SKILL.md lines 3, 10, and 14-16 do not contain weak cryptographic algorithms. They are generic template descriptions and placeholder capability labels.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain no crypto APIs, algorithms, hashes, ciphers, or key handling. The static matches are false positives.

content.value_statement: Teams need a starting point for repeatable AI workflow skills. This template shows how to define triggers, capabilities, resources, examples, and validation guidance.
actual_capabilities: Provides a frontmatter example with name, description, version, and allowed tools.
Shows how to describe skill capabilities and automatic invocation conditions.
Documents a pattern for referencing scripts, references, and assets from a skill directory.
Includes example workflows for configuration review and documentation lookup.
Encourages input validation and clear guidance when scripts are used.
limitations: The skill contains placeholder tasks and must be customized before production use.
Referenced scripts, references, and assets are not included in this package.
The template grants Bash access, so script use requires careful review.
It does not implement a complete domain-specific workflow by itself.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Use the structure as a starting point for a team-specific automation skill.

use_cases.title: Document Skill Activation Rules
use_cases.description: Model when an AI assistant should invoke a skill based on file changes or user questions.

use_cases.title: Plan Validation Workflows
use_cases.description: Outline how configuration files could be analyzed, validated, and improved.

prompt_templates.title: Customize the Description
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the placeholders in this skill with a concrete workflow for my team. Keep the structure simple and specific.

prompt_templates.title: Define Invocation Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Create precise activation rules for this skill based on these file types, user requests, and project signals.

prompt_templates.title: Add Resource Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the resource section so it references only files and scripts that exist in this skill package.

prompt_templates.title: Harden Script Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the Bash and helper-script guidance. Recommend safe argument handling, validation checks, and approval boundaries.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for reviewing configuration files.
output_examples.output: A completed skill outline with a focused description, invocation triggers, validation steps, resource references, and user-facing examples.

output_examples.input: Review this template before publishing it.
output: Script execution guidance that needs review.

output_examples.input: Make the activation rules more specific.
output_examples.output: A shorter trigger list tied to exact file extensions, commands, and user intents, with broad placeholder wording removed.

best_practices: Replace every placeholder with a concrete task, tool, or domain before publishing.
Include any referenced scripts, references, or assets so reviewers can inspect them.
Limit Bash usage to reviewed commands with clear inputs and validation.
anti_patterns: Do not publish the template with task X, domain Z, or situation W unchanged.
Do not reference helper scripts that are missing from the package.
Do not allow broad automatic invocation without precise user or file signals.

faq.question: Is this a complete production skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a template that needs concrete capabilities, triggers, and resources before use.

faq.question: Does it include working helper scripts?
faq.answer: No. The package only includes SKILL.md, although it references scripts, references, and assets.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill allows Bash and instructs script execution, but the scripts are not included for review.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should authors customize first?
faq.answer: Authors should replace the description, capabilities, invocation rules, and resource paths.

faq.question: What should reviewers check before publication?
faq.answer: Reviewers should verify referenced files exist, inspect scripts, and confirm activation rules are narrow.
